Question: Half portion of a spherical capacitor is filled with a dielectric of dielectric constant k = 2 and conductivity σ. The charge given to spherical capacitor is Q0. Due to the conductivity of dielectric charge leaks. The time constant for the discharge circuit is 'x' time of $\frac{{{\varepsilon _0}}}{\sigma }.$ Find the value of x.
Solution:
Let Co be the capacitance of the two shells,

$$C = \frac{{{\varepsilon _0}A}}{{2d}} + \frac{{k{\varepsilon _0}A}}{{2d}} = \frac{{3{C_o}}}{2}$$

$$dR = \frac{{dx}}{{2\pi \sigma {x^2}}}$$

Integrate

$$R = \frac{{b - a}}{{2\pi \sigma ab}}$$

$$C = \frac{3}{2} \times \frac{{4\pi {\varepsilon _0}ab}}{{b - a}}$$

$$\bar C = RC = \frac{{b - a}}{{2\pi \sigma ab}} \times \frac{3}{2}\frac{{4\pi {\varepsilon _0}ab}}{{b - a}}$$

$$\bar C = RC = \frac{{3{\varepsilon _0}}}{\sigma }$$

$$\therefore \boxed{x = 3}$$

Question: Two simple pendulums of length 5 m and 20 m respectively are given small linear displacements in one direction at the same time. They will again be in phase after how many minimum number of complete oscillations of the pendulum of shorter length?
Solution:
If t be the time taken by pendulums to come in same phase again first time after $t = 0$.
and
${N_S} = $ Number of oscillations made by shorter length pendulum with time period ${T_S}$.
${N_L} = $ Number of oscillations made by longer length pendulum with time period ${T_L}$.
Then 
$t = {N_S}{T_S} = {N_L}{T_L}$
$ \Rightarrow $ ${N_S}2\pi \sqrt {\frac{5}{g}} = {N_L} \times 2\pi \sqrt {\frac{{20}}{g}} $ ($\because T = 2\pi \sqrt {\frac{l}{g}} $)
$ \Rightarrow $ ${N_S} = 2{N_L}$ i.e. if ${N_L} = 1$ $ \Rightarrow $ ${N_S} = 2$

Question: The upper half of an inclined plane of inclination θ is perfectly smooth while the lower half is rough. A block starting from rest at the top of the plane will again come to rest at the bottom if the coefficient of friction between the block and the lower half of the plane is given by \[\mu = k\,\tan \theta \], where k is:
Solution:
The acceleration of the block while it is sliding down the upper half of the inclined plane is $$g\,sin θ$$.
If  $$μ$$ is the coefficient of kinetic friction between the block and the lower half of the plane, the retardation of the block while it is sliding down the lower half is  $$-{\text{ }}\left( {g{\text{ }}sin{\text{ }}\theta {\text{ }}-{\text{ }}\mu g{\text{ }}cos{\text{ }}\theta } \right).$$
For the block to come to rest at the bottom of the inclined plane, the acceleration in the first half must be equal to the retardation in the second half, i.e.
$$g\sin \theta  =  - \left( {g\sin \theta  - \mu g\cos \theta } \right)$$
or μ cos θ = 2 sin θ
μ = 2 tan θ

Question: How many molecules of acetone will be required to form mesitylene through aldol condensation?

Two ideal solenoids of same dimensions are shown in figure. One is air cored with 600 turns while other is Aluminium cored with 200 turns (relative permeability of Aluminium is 3), are connected in a circuit as shown in the figure. The switch S is closed at t = 0. Find the ratio of potential difference across air cored solenoid to that of Aluminium cored solenoid at any time t. (ignore mutual inductance)
The dot product of two vectors \[ - 2\,\widehat i + 3\,\widehat j\, + \,\widehat k\,\,{\text{and}}\,\,2\,\widehat i + 2\,\widehat j\, - 4\,\widehat k\] is – n, then what is the value of n
Solution:
Let \[\overrightarrow A = - 2\,\widehat i + 3\,\widehat j\, + \,\widehat k,\,\,\overrightarrow B = 2\,\widehat i + 2\,\widehat j\, - 4\,\widehat k\,\]

\[\overrightarrow A \cdot \overrightarrow B = - 2 \times 2 + 3 \times 2 + 1 \times \left( { - 4} \right) = - 2\]

Question: A block of mass m placed on a rough inclined plane of inclination θ = 30o can be just prevented from sliding down by applying a force F1 up the plane and it can be made to just slide up the plane by applying a force F2 up the plane. If the coefficient of friction between the block and the inclined plane is  \[\frac{1}{2\sqrt{3}}\]  , then, \[\frac{{{F_2}}}{{{F_1}}}\]is [Take g = 10 m/s2]
Solution:
The force F1 required to prevent the block from sliding down is

$${F_1} = mg\sin \theta  - \mu \,mg\cos \theta $$                ...(i)

The force F2 required to make the block move up the plane is

$${F_2} = mg\sin \theta  + \mu \,mg\cos \theta $$            ...(ii)

From Eqs. (i) and (ii) we get

$${F_2} + {F_1} = 2mg\sin \theta $$

and $${F_2} - {F_1} = 2 \mu mg\cos \theta $$

Dividing the two equations, we get

$$\frac{{{F_2} + {F_1}}}{{{F_2} - {F_1}}} = \frac{{\tan \theta }}{\mu } = \frac{{\tan {{30}^ \circ }}}{{1/(2\,\sqrt 3) }} = 2$$

Which gives $${F_2} = 3{F_1}.$$

Question: How many chiral stereoisomers are possible for 2-bromo-3-chlorobutane?
Solution: Since the compound has two different chiral carbons, therefore, 22 = 4 optical isomers are possible.

Question:
If $f\left( x \right) = \left\{ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  {\frac{{{{\left( {\exp \left\{ {\left( {x + 3} \right)\ln 27} \right\}} \right)}^{\frac{1}{{27}}\left[ {x + 1} \right]}} - 9}}{{{3^x} - 27}}}&;&{x < 3} \\
  {\lambda .\frac{{1 - \cos \left( {x - 3} \right)}}{{\left( {x - 3} \right)\tan \left( {x - 3} \right)}}}&;&{x > 3}
\end{array}} \right.$ is continuous at x = 3, then the value of $8100\lambda $ must be
Solution:
RHL = $\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to {3^ + }} \,f\left( x \right) = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} \,f\left( {3 + h} \right) = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} \,\lambda .\left( {\frac{{1 - \cos \,h}}{{h\tan \,h}}} \right)$
$\begin{gathered}
   = \lambda \,\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} \,\frac{{\frac{{1 - \cos \,h}}{{{h^2}}}}}{{\left( {\frac{{\tan \,h}}{h}} \right)}} \\
   = \lambda \frac{{\left( {\frac{1}{2}} \right)}}{1} \\
   = \frac{\lambda }{2} \\
\end{gathered} $
LHL = \[\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to {3^ - }} \,\,f\left( x \right) = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} \,f\left( {3 - h} \right)\]
\[\begin{gathered}
  \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} \,\frac{{{{\left( {\exp \left\{ {\left( {6 - h} \right)\ln 27} \right\}} \right)}^{\frac{1}{{27}}\left[ {4 - h} \right]}} - 9}}{{{3^{3 - h}} - 27}} \\
  \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\, = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} \,\frac{{{{\left( {\exp \left\{ {\left( {6 - h} \right)\ln 27} \right\}} \right)}^{\frac{1}{9}}} - 9}}{{{3^3}\left( {{3^{ - h}} - 1} \right)}} \\
\end{gathered} \]
$\begin{gathered}
   = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} \,\frac{{{{\left( {{{27}^{6 - h}}} \right)}^{1/9}} - 9}}{{27\left( {{3^{ - h}} - 1} \right)}} \\
   = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} \,\frac{{{{\left( {{3^{\left( {18 - 3h} \right)}}} \right)}^{1/9}} - 9}}{{27\left( {{3^{ - h}} - 1} \right)}} \\
   = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} \,\frac{{\left[ {{3^2}\left( {{3^{ - h/3}} - 1} \right)/\left( { - h/3} \right)} \right].\left( { - h/3} \right)}}{{\left[ {27\left( {{3^{ - h}} - 1} \right)/\left( { - h} \right)} \right].\left( { - h} \right)}} \\
   = \frac{1}{9} \\
\end{gathered} $
⸪ f(x) is continuous at x = 3
⸪ LHL = RHL
$\begin{gathered}
   \Rightarrow \,\,\frac{1}{9} = \frac{\lambda }{2} \\
   \Rightarrow \lambda = \frac{2}{9} \\
\end{gathered} $
Then, $8100\lambda = 8100 \times \frac{2}{9} = 1800$

Question: Two wires are made of the same material and have the same volume. However, wire 1 has cross-sectional area A and wire 2 has cross-sectional area 3A. If the length of wire 1 increases by $\Delta x$on applying force F, the force needed to stretch wire 2 by equal amount (or elongation) is nF, where n is:
Solution:
Let L1 and L2 be the lengths of wires 1 and 2

Given $${V_1} = {V_2} \Rightarrow A{L_1} = 3A{L_2} \Rightarrow \frac{{{L_1}}}{{{L_2}}} = 3$$

If F and F’ are the forces applied to 1 and 2,

$$Y = \frac{{F{L_1}}}{{A\Delta x}} = \frac{{F'{L_2}}}{{3A\Delta x}} \Rightarrow \frac{{F'}}{F} = \frac{{3{L_1}}}{{{L_2}}} = 3 \times 3 = 9$$

Question: If the solution of the differential equation \[y' + \frac{y}{x} - \sqrt y  = 0,y\left( 1 \right) = 0\] is \[y = f\left( x \right)\] (not identically 0), then \[\left[ {9f\left( 9 \right)} \right]\] is equal to (where [.] greatest integer function).
Solution:
\[y' + \frac{1}{x}y = {y^{\frac{1}{2}}}\]
\[ \Rightarrow {y^{\frac{{ - 1}}{2}}}y' + \frac{1}{x}{y^{\frac{1}{2}}} = 1\]
Put \[{y^{\frac{1}{2}}} = v \Rightarrow v' = \frac{1}{2}{y^{\frac{{ - 1}}{2}}}y'\]
\[2v' + \frac{1}{x}v = 1 \Rightarrow v' + \frac{1}{{2x}}v = \frac{1}{2}\]
Here \[P = \frac{1}{{2x}},Q = \frac{1}{2}\]
If \[ = {e^{\int {Pdx} }} = {e^{\int {\frac{1}{{2x}}dx} }} = {e^{\frac{1}{2}\ln \left| x \right|}} = {\left| x \right|^{^{\frac{1}{2}}}}\]
So, \[v \times IF = \int {IF} \times Q\]
\[v \times {\left| x \right|^{\frac{1}{2}}} = \int {{{\left| x \right|}^{\frac{1}{2}}}} \times \frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow {y^{\frac{1}{2}}}{\left| x \right|^{\frac{1}{2}}} = \frac{{2{{\left| x \right|}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{3} \times \frac{1}{2} + c\]
Now, \[y\left( 1 \right) = 0 \Rightarrow 0 = \frac{1}{3} + c \Rightarrow c = - \frac{1}{3}\]
\[\therefore y = {\left( {\frac{1}{3}x - \frac{1}{3}{x^{\frac{{ - 1}}{2}}}} \right)^2} \Rightarrow y = \frac{{{x^3} - 2{x^{\frac{3}{2}}} + 1}}{{9x}}\]
\[ \Rightarrow y = \frac{1}{9}\left( {{x^2} - 2{x^{\frac{1}{2}}} + \frac{1}{x}} \right)\]
\[\therefore \left[ {9f\left( 9 \right)} \right] = 75\]

Question: The range of a projectile, when launched at $75^o$ with the horizontal, is 0.5 km. What will be its range (in km) when launched at $$45^o$$ (assume ground to ground projectile launched from a fixed point on a horizontal ground)?
The range of projectile when fired at 75<sup>o</sup> with the horizontal is 0.5 km. What will be its range (in km) when fired at 45<sup>o.</sup>
Solution:
\[{R_1} = \frac{{{u^2}\sin 2\theta }}{g} = \frac{{{u^2}\sin 150^\circ }}{g}\]

\[\begin{gathered}
   = \frac{{{u^2}\sin 30^\circ }}{g} = \frac{{{u^2}}}{{2g}} \\
   = \frac{{{u^2}\sin 90^\circ }}{g} = \frac{{{u^2}}}{g} \\
  {\text{Therefore}}\,\,{R_2} = 2{R_1} = 2 \times 0.5 = 1{\text{ km}}. \\
\end{gathered} \]

Question: The number of arrangements of letters the word ARROW, in which the two R's do not appear together is equal to _____.
Solution:
Given word is ARROW, that is A, RR, O, W.
Total number of arrangements is $\frac{{5!}}{{2!}} = 60$.
Now, considering RR as one object, number of arrangements is $4! = 24$.
So, required number of arrangements is $60 - 24 = 36$.

Question: A thin glass plate of area A and mass m is hinged along one of its sides. The speed with which the air should be blown parallel to its upper surface to hold the plate horizontal is \[\gamma \sqrt {\frac{{mg}}{{18pA}}} \]. Find the value of γ . The density of air is p
Solution:
Applying Bernoulli’s theorem for above the plate (2) and below the plate (1), we have
\[\begin{gathered}
  {p_1} = {p_2} + \frac{1}{2}p{v^2} \\
   \Rightarrow {p_1} - {p_2} = \frac{1}{2}p{v^2} \\
\end{gathered} \]
Upward force is \[\left( {{p_1} - {p_2}} \right)A = \frac{1}{2}p{v^2}A\]
\[\begin{gathered}
  \therefore \, & \frac{1}{2}p{v^2}A = mg \\
   \Rightarrow & v = \sqrt {\frac{{2mg}}{{pA}}} = 6\sqrt {\frac{{mg}}{{18pA}}} \\
   \Rightarrow & \gamma = 6 \\
\end{gathered} \]

Question: An unknown compound (A) (molar mass = 180) on acylation gives a product (molar mass = 390) then find the number of hydroxyl group present in compound (A).
Solution:
Number or Hydroxyl group $n = \frac{{m' - m}}{{42}}$
Where
$m'$= mass of product
m = unknown compound
$n = \frac{{390 - 180}}{{42}} = 5$

Question:
How many of the following are condensation polymer?
Nylon-6, 6, Terylene, Neoprene, Teflon, Acrylonitrile, Dacron, Buna-S
Solution: Neoprene, Acrylonitrile, Buna-S and Teflon are addition polymers

Question: An ideal monoatomic gas undergoes a process in which its internal energy U and density \[\rho \] vary as \[{U\rho }\] = constant. The ratio of change in internal energy to the work done by the gas is (0.5x). Find value of x.
Solution:
\[{U\rho } = {\text{constant}}\]
\[\therefore \frac{T}{V} = {\text{constant}}\]
\[\therefore P = {\text{constant}}\]
\[\frac{{{\text{dU}}}}{{dW}} = \frac{{dU}}{{dQ - dU}} = \frac{{{C_V}}}{{{C_p } - {C_V}}}\]
\[ = \frac{1}{{({C_p } / {C_V} )- 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left( {5/3} \right) - 1}} = \frac{3}{2}\]

Question:
If \[f\left( x \right) = \left\{ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  {\frac{{\tan \left[ {{x^2}} \right]\pi }}{{a{x^2}}} + a{x^3} + b}&{,\,\,\,\,0 \leqslant x \leqslant 1} \\
  {2\cos \pi x + {{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}&{,\,\,1 < x \leqslant 2}
\end{array}} \right.\] is differentiable in [0, 2], then \[b = \frac{\pi }{4} - \frac{{26}}{{{k}}}\]. Find \[\frac{1}{a^2} + k^2\] {where [ ] denotes greatest integer function}.
Solution:
\[f\left( x \right) = \left\{ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  {a{x^3} + b}&,&{0 \leqslant x \leqslant 1} \\
  {2\cos \pi x + {{\tan }^{ - 1}x}}&,&{1 < x \leqslant 2}
\end{array}} \right.\]
\[f'\left( x \right) = \left\{ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  {3a{x^2}}&,&{0 < x < 1} \\
  { - 2\pi \sin \pi x + \frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}}&,&{1 < x < 2}
\end{array}} \right.\]
As the function is differentiable in [0, 2]
         \[ \Rightarrow \]function is differentiable at x = 1
\[\therefore \,\,f'\left( {{1^ - }} \right) = f'\left( {{1^ + }} \right)\]
\[\begin{gathered}
   \Rightarrow \,\,\,3a = \frac{1}{2} \\
   \Rightarrow \,\,a = \frac{1}{6} \\
\end{gathered} \]
Function will also be continuous at x = 1
\[\therefore \,\,\,\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to {1^ - }} \,\,f\left( x \right) = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to {1^ + }} \,\,f\left( x \right)\]
\[ \Rightarrow \,\,a + b = - 2 + \frac{\pi }{4}\]
     \[\therefore \,\,\,b = - 2 - \frac{1}{6} + \frac{\pi }{4} = \frac{\pi }{4} - \frac{{13}}{6} \Rightarrow {k} = 12\]

Question: Which of the following carbocation can undergo rearrangement:

Ti is purified by following method:
${\text{Ti}}\left( {{\text{impure}}} \right) + {I_2}\xrightarrow[\Delta ]{}\mathop {Ti{I_x}}\limits_{{\text{vapour}}} \xrightarrow[\Delta ]{}{\text{Pure}}\,Ti + {I_2}$
Find the value of x.
Solution: $\rm Ti + 2{I_2}\xrightarrow[\Delta ]{}Ti{I_4}$

Question: A pendulum has time period T in air. When it is made to oscillate in water, it acquired a time period T′ = √ 2T . The specific gravity of the pendulum bob is equal to
Solution:
The effective acceleration of a bob in water = g′ = g\[\left( {1 - \frac{d}{D}} \right)\] where d & D are the density of water & the bob respectively.

\[\frac{D}{d}\] = specific gravity of the bob.

     Since the period of oscillation of the bob in air & water are given as

          T= 2π \[\sqrt {\frac{\ell }{g}} \] & T′ = 2π \[\sqrt {\frac{\ell }{{g'}}} \]

     ∴ T/T′ =\[\sqrt {\frac{{g'}}{g}} \]=\[\sqrt {\frac{{g(1 - d/D)}}{g}} \] = \[\sqrt {1 - \frac{d}{D}} \] = \[\sqrt {1 - \frac{1}{s}} \]

     Putting T/T′ = 1/√ 2, we obtain

          1/2 = 1 – 1/s ⇒ \[\frac{1}{s} = \frac{1}{2}\] ⇒ s = 2

Question: A square of elastic sheet of dimension (a × a) has a mass 125 gm. A force of 2.5 N is applied to each of the four edges. What is the velocity (in m/s) of waves on the sheet? a = 0.75 m. (Give the answer as the nearest integer).

The number of distinct real values of $\lambda $ for which the vectors \[ - {\lambda ^2}\hat i\, + \,\hat j\, + \,\hat k,\,\,\hat i\, - \,{\lambda ^2}\hat j\, + \,\hat k\] and \[\hat i\, + \,\hat j\, - \,{\lambda ^2}\hat k\] are coplanar is
Solution:
Since given vectors are coplanar
\[ \Rightarrow \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  { - {\lambda ^2}}&1&1 \\
  1&{ - {\lambda ^2}}&1 \\
  1&1&{ - {\lambda ^2}}
\end{array}} \right| = 0\]
\[\left( {2 - {\lambda ^2}} \right)\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  1&0&0 \\
  1&{ - \left( {{\lambda ^2} + 1} \right)}&0 \\
  1&0&{ - \left( {{\lambda ^2} + 1} \right)}
\end{array}} \right| = 0\]
\[ \Rightarrow {\lambda ^2} = 2\]
\[ \Rightarrow \;\lambda = \pm \sqrt 2 \]

Question: The sum total of number of lone pair of electrons on central atom XeF<sub>2</sub>, XeF<sub>4</sub> and XeF<sub>6</sub> is
Solution:

Question: A body of mass 0.4 kg starting at origin at \[t = 0\] with a speed of \[10m{s^{ - 1}}\] in the positive \[x - axis\] direction is subjected to a constant \[F = 8N\] towards negative\[x - axis\]. The position of the body after 25 s is –n x (1000) m. Then n is
Solution:
Here,
Mass of the particle, \[m = 0.4kg\]
\[F = - 8N\](minus sign for direction of force)
\[\therefore \] Acceleration, \[a = a = \frac{F}{m} = \frac{{ - 8N}}{{0.4kg}} = - 20m{s^{ - 2}}\]
The position of the body at any time \[t\] is given by
\[x = {x_0} + ut + \frac{1}{2}a{t^2}\] The position of the body at \[t = 0\] is 0, therefore \[{x_0} = 0.\]
\[\therefore \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,x = ut + \frac{1}{2}a{t^2}\]
Position of the body at \[t = 25s\]
Here, \[u = 10m{s^{ - 1}},a = - 20m{s^{ - 2}},t = 25s\]
\[\begin{gathered}
  x = 10 \times 25 + \frac{1}{2}\left( { - 20} \right){\left( {25} \right)^2} \\
   = 250 - 6250 = - 6000\,m \\
\end{gathered} \]

Question: The acceleration due to gravity is found upto an accuracy of 4% on a planet. The energy supplied to a simple pendulum to known mass ‘m’ to undertake oscillations of time period T is being estimated. If time period is measured to an accuracy of 3%, the accuracy to which the energy measured is known as .......%
Solution:
$\begin{gathered}
  {\text{T}} = 2\pi \sqrt {\frac{\ell }{{\text{g}}}} \Rightarrow \ell = \frac{{{{\text{T}}^2}\;{\text{g}}}}{{4{\pi ^2}}} \\
  {\text{E}} = \operatorname{mg} \ell \frac{{{\theta ^2}}}{2} = {\text{m}}{{\text{g}}^2}\frac{{{{\text{T}}^2}{\theta ^2}}}{{8{\pi ^2}}} \\
  \frac{{{\text{dE}}}}{{\text{E}}} = 2\left( {\frac{{{\text{dg}}}}{{\text{g}}} + \frac{{{\text{dT}}}}{{\text{T}}}} \right) \\
   = 2(4 + 3) = 14\% \\
\end{gathered} $

Question: A solid cylinder of mass m and radius r starts rolling down an inclined plane of inclination $\theta $. Friction is enough to prevent slipping. Find the value of s if the speed of its center of mass is \[\sqrt {\frac{n}{3}gh} \], when its center of mass when its center of mass has fallen a height h.
Solution:
Considering the two shown positions of the cylinder. As it does not slip hence total mechanical energy will be conserved.
Energy at position 1 is ${E_1} = mgh$
Energy at position 2 is ${E_2} = \frac{1}{2}m{v_{COM}}^2 + \frac{1}{2}{I_{COM\,}}{\omega ^2}$
$\frac{{{v_{COM}}}}{r} = \omega \,\,{\text{and}}\,\,{I_{COM}} = \frac{{m{r^2}}}{2}$
$ \Rightarrow \,\,{E_2} = \frac{3}{4}m{v_{COM}}^2$
From conservation of energy, ${E_1} = {E_2}\,\,{\text{or}}\,\,mgh = \frac{3}{4}m{v_{COM}}^2$
$ \Rightarrow \,\,{v_{COM}} = \sqrt {\frac{4}{3}gh} $

Question: Six identical parallel metallic large plates are located in air at equal distances d to neighbouring plates. The area of each plate is A. Some of the plates are connected by conducting wires to each other. The capacitance of the system of plates between two points P and Q in pF is: (Take A = 0.05 m2, d = 17.7 mm, ε0 = 8.85 × 10–12 F/m).
Solution:
The equivalent capacitance between points PQ is capacitance between two neighboring plates by
wheat stone bridge.
\[{C_{eq}} = \frac{{{\varepsilon _0}A}}{d} = \frac{{8.85 \times {{10}^{ - 12}} \times 0.05}}{{17.7 \times {{10}^{ - 3}}}}\,F = 25\,pF.\]

Question: <!-StartFragment->If $f\left( x \right) = \frac{{3{x^2} + 1}}{{\sqrt {{x^4} + {x^2}} }}\forall \,\,0 < x \leqslant \sqrt 2 $, then $f\left( x \right)$ has minimum value $M$ at $x = m$. Then, $\left( {{M^2} + \frac{1}{{{m^2}}}} \right)$ is equal to<!-EndFragment->
Solution:
$\because \frac{{2x}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 1} }} + \frac{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 1} }}{x} \geqslant 2\sqrt {\frac{{2x}}{{\sqrt {{x^2}} + 1}} \times \frac{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 1} }}{x}} = 2\sqrt 2 $
$\therefore M = 2\sqrt 2 \& m = \pm 1,\,\,$i.e., $m = 1$

Question:
Let \[f\left( x \right) = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  {ax + 3,}&{x < 1} \\
  {{x^2} + bx,}&{x \geqslant 1}
\end{array}} \right.\], be a continuous function and 
\[g(x) = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}}
  {{x^2} + 3x + 2,}&{x > 0} \\
  {2x + 1,}&{x < 0}
\end{array}} \right.\], be a function such that $\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} f\left( {g\left( x \right)} \right)$ exists and equal to c, then $\left| {a + b - c} \right|$ equals
Solution:
y = f(x) is continuous
⇒ a + 3 = b + 1 ⇒ a + 2 = b ….…(1)
$\begin{gathered}
  L.H.L = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to {0^ - }} f\left( {g\left( x \right)} \right) \\
   = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to {1^ - }} f\left( x \right) = a + 3 \\
\end{gathered} $
$\begin{gathered}
  R.H.L. = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to {0^ + }} f\left( {g\left( x \right)} \right) \\
   = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to {2^ + }} f\left( x \right) = 4 + 2b \\
\end{gathered} $
L.H.L. = R. H. L. = c
⇒ a = 2b + 1 ….(2)
From (1) and (2)
b = -3, a = -5 and c = -2.

Question: The peak value of an alternating emf E is given by $E = {E_0}\cos \omega \,t$is 10 volts and its frequency is 50 Hz. At time$t = \frac{1}{{600}}s$, the instantaneous emf is $n\sqrt 3 \,V$, where n is:
Solution:
$E = {E_0}\cos \omega t = {E_0}\cos (2\pi \nu t)$
$ = 10\cos \frac{{2\pi \times 50 \times 1}}{{600}} = 10\;\cos \frac{\pi }{6}$$ = 5\sqrt 3 \;volt.$
